content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jean Sternenberg 
city Clerk 
March 13, 1986 
City Hall - Administrative Offices 
P.O. Box 578 
Healdsburg, CA 
95448 
Re: 
FPPC No.~-86-074 
Dear MS. Sternenberg: 
Thank you for your letter dated February 21, 1986, 
advising us that Councilman William Proctor is unable to 
file an annual Statement of Economic Interests due to health 
problems. 
We have made a note of this in our files, and will 
not expect a Statement from Mr. Proctor until we hear from 
you. 
Sincerely, 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Division Chief, 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


February 21, 1986 
Mr. Bob Leidigh 
Legal Department 
California Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
CITY OF HEALDSBURG 
,city Han -
Administrative Offices 
~. 
.,. 
~"p:o. Box 578 
&!$6 Mat~son Street 
vtle!I8~lWi;, CA 95448 
(707) 433-9425 
This is to advise you that Mr. William Proctor, Councilman 
for the City of Healdsburg, will not be able to submit the 
Annual Statement of Economic Interest (Form No. 721) due to 
health reasons. 
Mr. Proctor has suffered a stroke and he is, at this time, 
paralyzed. 
If you need additional information, do not 
hesitate to contact this office. 
Very truly yours, 
JS;mc 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jean sternenberg 
city of Healdsburg 
P. O. Box 578 
Healdsburg, CA 
95448 
Dear Ms. sternenburg: 
March 4, 1986 
Re: 
A-86-074 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours I.e 
/~/~ 
" 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance 
Division 
and Analysis 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 9S804~0807 • 
(916) 122~5660

sections.question_synthetic: Can Councilman William Proctor be excused from filing an Annual Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) due to health problems, specifically a recent stroke that has left him paralyzed?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC agreed not to expect the Annual Statement of Economic Interests from Councilman Proctor and noted the health-related exemption in their files.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (February 21, 1986) and FPPC response (March 13, 1986). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections, but the substantive legal question and answer are clearly identifiable from the correspondence.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can Councilman William Proctor be excused from filing an Annual Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) due to health problems, specifically a recent stroke that has left him paralyzed?

CONCLUSION: We have made a note of this in our files, and will not expect a Statement from Mr. Proctor until we hear from you.

embedding.summary: The City Clerk of Healdsburg requested an exemption from filing an Annual Statement of Economic Interests for Councilman William Proctor due to his stroke and resulting paralysis. The FPPC granted the request and noted it in their files.
